subject: UX & Research
title: Consistent Navigation Patterns
description: A micro-lesson that makes Consistent Navigation Patterns usable.

hook.text: Page 1: menu on top. Page 2: menu on the left. Page 3: menu at the bottom. Users relearn every page.

buildup.text: Consistency cuts cognitive load. When navigation stays put, users build spatial memory and move faster.

discovery.text: Jakob's Law: users spend most time on other sites. They expect yours to work like the ones they know.

twist.text: Consistency extends beyond position. If 'Save' is blue on one page and green on another, users doubt it.

climax.text: Design systems enforce consistency. Shared components ensure buttons and menus behave the same everywhere.

punchline.text: Same place, same look, same behavior. Consistency is cognitive access.

quiz.question: Why is navigation consistency an accessibility issue?
options: Inconsistent layouts force users to relearn navigation on every page, increasing cognitive load
Consistent navigation is only important for mobile users
Navigation patterns don't affect users with cognitive disabilities
